```python
from __future__ import annotations

from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
from typing import List, Optional


class ListingLinks(BaseModel):
    listing_urls: List[str] = Field(default_factory=list)


class ProductImage(BaseModel):
    url: str
    alt: Optional[str] = None


class ProductCard(BaseModel):
    source_site: str
    product_url: str
    title: str
    gender: Optional[str] = None  # female / male / unisex / unknown
    category: Optional[str] = None
    subcategory: Optional[str] = None
    season: List[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
    description: str = ""
    pattern_info: str = ""
    garment_elements: List[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
    materials: List[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
    adult_only: bool = True
    is_accessory: bool = False
    is_child_item: bool = False
    images: List[ProductImage] = Field(default_factory=list)
    raw_text: str = ""


class GeneratedTags(BaseModel):
    tags: List[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
```

### `src/pattern_scraper/utils.py`
```python
from __future__ import annotations

import json
import re
from pathlib import Path
from urllib.parse import urlparse


def ensure_dir(path: Path) -> None:
    path.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)


def read_json(path: Path, default):
    if not path.exists():
        return default
    return json.loads(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))


def write_json(path: Path, data) -> None:
    path.write_text(json.dumps(data, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")


def append_jsonl(path: Path, row: dict) -> None:
    with path.open("a", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        f.write(json.dumps(row, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n")


def read_jsonl_set(path: Path, key: str) -> set[str]:
    if not path.exists():
        return set()
    result = set()
    with path.open("r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        for line in f:
            line = line.strip()
            if not line:
                continue
            try:
                obj = json.loads(line)
                value = obj.get(key)
                if isinstance(value, str) and value:
                    result.add(value)
            except Exception:
                continue
    return result


def slugify(text: str, max_len: int = 120) -> str:
    text = text.lower().strip()
    text = re.sub(r"[^\w\s-]+", " ", text, flags=re.UNICODE)
    text = re.sub(r"[\s_-]+", "-", text, flags=re.UNICODE).strip("-")
    return text[:max_len] or "item"


def stable_item_id(url: str, title: str) -> str:
    host = urlparse(url).netloc.replace("www.", "")
    return f"{slugify(host)}__{slugify(title or url)}"


def is_probably_image_url(url: str) -> bool:
    u = url.lower()
    return any(ext in u for ext in [".jpg", ".jpeg", ".png", ".webp", ".avif"])


def dedupe_preserve_order(items: list[str]) -> list[str]:
    seen = set()
    out = []
    for item in items:
        if item and item not in seen:
            seen.add(item)
            out.append(item)
    return out
```

```python
from __future__ import annotations

from pathlib import Path
from pattern_scraper.utils import ensure_dir, append_jsonl, read_jsonl_set


class JsonlState:
    def __init__(self, state_dir: Path):
        ensure_dir(state_dir)
        self.state_dir = state_dir
        self.processed_pages_file = state_dir / "processed_pages.jsonl"
        self.saved_items_file = state_dir / "saved_items.jsonl"
        self.discovered_links_file = state_dir / "discovered_links.jsonl"

    def processed_pages(self) -> set[str]:
        return read_jsonl_set(self.processed_pages_file, "url")

    def saved_items(self) -> set[str]:
        return read_jsonl_set(self.saved_items_file, "item_id")

    def discovered_links(self) -> set[str]:
        return read_jsonl_set(self.discovered_links_file, "url")

    def mark_processed_page(self, url: str) -> None:
        append_jsonl(self.processed_pages_file, {"url": url})

    def mark_saved_item(self, item_id: str, url: str) -> None:
        append_jsonl(self.saved_items_file, {"item_id": item_id, "url": url})

    def add_discovered_link(self, url: str, site: str) -> None:
        append_jsonl(self.discovered_links_file, {"url": url, "site": site})
```

```python
def normalize_tag(tag: str) -> str:
    return tag.strip().lower().replace(" ", "-")


def merge_tags(*groups: List[str]) -> list[str]:
    seen = set()
    out = []
    for group in groups:
        for tag in group:
            t = normalize_tag(tag)
            if t and t not in seen:
                seen.add(t)
                out.append(t)
```

### `src/pattern_scraper/browser_agent.py`
```python
from __future__ import annotations

import os

from browser_use import Agent, Browser, ChatOpenAI
from dotenv import load_dotenv


def build_browser(allowed_domains: list[str]) -> Browser:
    return Browser(
        headless=True,
        allowed_domains=allowed_domains,
    )


def build_llm() -> ChatOpenAI:
    load_dotenv()
    api_key = os.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY")
    if not api_key:
        raise RuntimeError("OPENAI_API_KEY is not set in .env")
    return ChatOpenAI(
        model="o3",
        api_key=api_key,
    )


def build_agent(task: str, browser: Browser, output_model_schema=None) -> Agent:
    return Agent(
        task=task,
        llm=build_llm(),
        browser=browser,
        use_vision=True,
        output_model_schema=output_model_schema,
        max_failures=3,
        step_timeout=120,
    )
```

### `src/pattern_scraper/extract.py`
```python
from __future__ import annotations

import json
from typing import Iterable

from pattern_scraper.browser_agent import build_agent, build_browser
from pattern_scraper.config import SiteConfig
from pattern_scraper.models import ListingLinks, ProductCard, GeneratedTags
from pattern_scraper.utils import dedupe_preserve_order


def listing_task(site: SiteConfig, limit: int) -> str:
    return f"""
Open {site.start_url}
{site.listing_prompt}

Important rules:
- Stay only on the same site.
- You may browse category pages and pagination.
- Return up to {limit} unique product URLs.
- Do not return category pages or search result pages as final URLs.
- Prefer concrete pattern pages for adult garments.
- If the site has women/men categories, inspect both.
""".strip()


def product_task(url: str, site_key: str) -> str:
    return f"""
Open this product page: {url}

Extract structured data for this exact item.

Rules:
- This must be an adult clothing sewing pattern or garment construction page.
- Exclude children and accessories.
- Collect multiple product/gallery images of the finished garment if available.
- Prefer garment photos, not line drawings only.
- Put all useful human-readable page text into raw_text.
- Fill fields carefully; if unknown, leave empty string / empty list / null.
- product_url must be exactly: {url}
- source_site must be exactly: {site_key}
- adult_only = true only if it is clearly adult clothing.
- is_accessory = true for bags, hats, scarves, belts etc.
- is_child_item = true for children/baby/kids items.
""".strip()


def tags_task(card: ProductCard, allowed_tags: list[str]) -> str:
    payload = json.dumps(card.model_dump(mode="json"), ensure_ascii=False, indent=2)
    allowed = ", ".join(allowed_tags)
    return f"""
You are generating normalized tags for a sewing pattern item.

Use ONLY tags from this allowed vocabulary when possible:
{allowed}

Input item:
{payload}

Rules:
- 5 to 20 tags
- lowercase
- hyphen-separated
- prioritize garment type, silhouette, fit, details, season, gender, style
- no duplicates
- if a needed tag is absent from vocabulary, you may add a short normalized tag
""".strip()


async def discover_listing_urls(site: SiteConfig, limit: int) -> list[str]:
    browser = build_browser(site.allow_domains)
    agent = build_agent(
        task=listing_task(site, limit),
        browser=browser,
        output_model_schema=ListingLinks,
    )
    history = await agent.run()
    data = history.structured_output
    if not data:
        return []
    return dedupe_preserve_order(data.listing_urls)[:limit]


async def extract_product(site: SiteConfig, url: str) -> ProductCard | None:
    browser = build_browser(site.allow_domains)
    agent = build_agent(
        task=product_task(url, site.key),
        browser=browser,
        output_model_schema=ProductCard,
    )
    history = await agent.run()
    data = history.structured_output
    if not data:
        return None
    return data


async def generate_tags(card: ProductCard, allowed_tags: list[str]) -> list[str]:
    browser = build_browser(["*"])
    agent = build_agent(
        task=tags_task(card, allowed_tags),
        browser=browser,
        output_model_schema=GeneratedTags,
    )
    history = await agent.run()
    data = history.structured_output
    if not data:
        return []
    return dedupe_preserve_order(data.tags)


def filter_valid_product(card: ProductCard) -> bool:
    if card.is_accessory or card.is_child_item:
        return False
    title = (card.title or "").lower()
    text = " ".join([
        title,
        card.category or "",
        card.subcategory or "",
        card.description or "",
        card.pattern_info or "",
        card.raw_text or "",
    ]).lower()

    banned = [
        "kids", "child", "children", "baby", "teen",
        "accessory", "bag", "backpack", "hat", "cap", "scarf",
        "sock", "gloves", "toy", "doll", "pet",
    ]
    if any(b in text for b in banned):
        return False

    clothing_positive = [
        "dress", "shirt", "blouse", "top", "skirt", "pants", "trousers",
        "jacket", "coat", "blazer", "hoodie", "sweater", "vest", "jumpsuit",
        "плать", "юбк", "брюк", "брюки", "рубаш", "блуз", "топ",
        "пиджак", "жакет", "пальто", "куртк", "худи", "комбинез",
    ]
    return any(w in text for w in clothing_positive)
```

### `src/pattern_scraper/downloads.py`
```python
from __future__ import annotations

import asyncio
from pathlib import Path
from urllib.parse import urlparse
import httpx

from pattern_scraper.utils import ensure_dir, is_probably_image_url


def filename_from_url(url: str, fallback: str) -> str:
    path = urlparse(url).path.strip("/")
    name = Path(path).name or fallback
    if "." not in name:
        name = f"{name}.jpg"
    return name[:180]


async def download_one(client: httpx.AsyncClient, url: str, target: Path) -> bool:
    try:
        r = await client.get(url, timeout=60.0, follow_redirects=True)
        r.raise_for_status()
        ctype = r.headers.get("content-type", "").lower()
        if "image" not in ctype and not is_probably_image_url(url):
            return False
        target.write_bytes(r.content)
        return True
    except Exception:
        return False


async def download_images(urls: list[str], out_dir: Path) -> list[str]:
    ensure_dir(out_dir)
    saved = []
    async with httpx.AsyncClient(headers={"User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0"}) as client:
        tasks = []
        targets = []
        for i, url in enumerate(urls, start=1):
            target = out_dir / filename_from_url(url, f"image_{i}.jpg")
            if target.exists():
                saved.append(str(target))
                continue
            targets.append(target)
            tasks.append(download_one(client, url, target))

        if tasks:
            results = await asyncio.gather(*tasks, return_exceptions=True)
            for ok, target in zip(results, targets):
                if ok is True and target.exists():
                    saved.append(str(target))
    return saved
```

```python
from __future__ import annotations

from pathlib import Path

from pattern_scraper.models import ProductCard
from pattern_scraper.utils import ensure_dir, write_json, stable_item_id


def save_item(base_dir: Path, card: ProductCard, tags: list[str], downloaded_images: list[str]) -> str:
    item_id = stable_item_id(card.product_url, card.title)
    item_dir = base_dir / item_id
    images_dir = item_dir / "images"
    ensure_dir(images_dir)

    write_json(item_dir / "metadata.json", {
        **card.model_dump(mode="json"),
        "downloaded_images": downloaded_images,
        "item_id": item_id,
    })
    write_json(item_dir / "tags.json", {"tags": tags})
    (item_dir / "raw_text.txt").write_text(card.raw_text or "", encoding="utf-8")

    return item_id
```

```python
from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import asyncio
from pathlib import Path

from pattern_scraper.config import DATA_DIR, STATE_DIR, ITEMS_DIR, SITES
from pattern_scraper.downloads import download_images
from pattern_scraper.extract import (
    discover_listing_urls,
    extract_product,
    filter_valid_product,
    generate_tags,
)
from pattern_scraper.state import JsonlState
from pattern_scraper.storage import save_item
from pattern_scraper.tags import BASE_TAGS, merge_tags
from pattern_scraper.utils import ensure_dir, stable_item_id


async def run_site(site_key: str, limit: int) -> None:
    site = SITES[site_key]
    ensure_dir(DATA_DIR)
    ensure_dir(STATE_DIR)
    ensure_dir(ITEMS_DIR)

    state = JsonlState(STATE_DIR)

    already_processed_pages = state.processed_pages()
    already_saved_items = state.saved_items()
    known_links = state.discovered_links()

    print(f"[{site_key}] discovering listing urls...")
    urls = await discover_listing_urls(site, limit=limit)

    fresh_urls = []
    for url in urls:
        if url not in known_links:
            state.add_discovered_link(url, site_key)
        if url not in already_processed_pages:
            fresh_urls.append(url)

    print(f"[{site_key}] discovered={len(urls)}, fresh={len(fresh_urls)}")

    for idx, url in enumerate(fresh_urls, start=1):
        print(f"[{site_key}] [{idx}/{len(fresh_urls)}] {url}")
        try:
            card = await extract_product(site, url)
            if not card:
                print("  -> no structured result")
                state.mark_processed_page(url)
                continue

            item_id = stable_item_id(card.product_url, card.title)
            if item_id in already_saved_items:
                print("  -> already saved")
                state.mark_processed_page(url)
                continue

            if not filter_valid_product(card):
                print("  -> filtered out (not adult clothing / accessory / child)")
                state.mark_processed_page(url)
                continue

            image_urls = [img.url for img in card.images if img.url]
            downloaded = await download_images(image_urls, ITEMS_DIR / item_id / "images")

            tags = await generate_tags(card, BASE_TAGS)
            tags = merge_tags(tags)

            saved_item_id = save_item(ITEMS_DIR, card, tags, downloaded)
            state.mark_saved_item(saved_item_id, url)
            state.mark_processed_page(url)
            print(f"  -> saved {saved_item_id}")

        except KeyboardInterrupt:
            print("\nStopped by user. Progress already saved.")
            raise
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"  -> error: {e}")
            # страницу не маркируем как processed при ошибке извлечения,
            # чтобы можно было повторить запуск


async def async_main():
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    parser.add_argument(
        "--sites",
        nargs="+",
        default=list(SITES.keys()),
        choices=list(SITES.keys()),
    )
    parser.add_argument("--limit-per-site", type=int, default=100)
    args = parser.parse_args()

    for site_key in args.sites:
        await run_site(site_key, limit=args.limit_per_site)


def main():
    asyncio.run(async_main())


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
```

## Senior ML engineer plan

### 1. Goal and success criteria

Build a **fresh standalone scraping pipeline** that:

- targets only these sites:
  - simplicity.com
  - vikisews.com
  - burdastyle.ru
  - helpersew.com
  - grasser.ru
  - shkatulka-sew.ru
  - korfiati.ru
  - marfy.it
- collects only:
  - **women’s and men’s clothing**
  - **no children’s items**
  - **no accessories**
- saves for each item:
  - separate item folder
  - multiple garment photos
  - `metadata.json`
  - source product URL
  - structured text fields: item title, category, season, design elements, useful page text
  - generated tags
- can be stopped safely and resumed later
- does not revisit already processed pages
- does not redownload already downloaded images

The core engineering principle is: **LLM-driven browser agent for discovery + extraction, deterministic local storage and resume logic for reliability**.

---

### 2. High-level architecture

Use a 4-stage pipeline:

#### Stage A. Discovery
For each domain, `browser-use` navigates category/search/listing pages and returns a list of **candidate product URLs** for adult clothing patterns only.

Output:
- `state/discovered_urls.jsonl`

#### Stage B. Product extraction
For each candidate URL not yet processed, `browser-use` opens the page and produces a structured object like:

- `title`
- `url`
- `source_site`
- `gender`
- `category`
- `subcategory`
- `season`
- `garment_elements`
- `pattern_description`
- `useful_text`
- `image_urls`
- `is_child_item`
- `is_accessory`
- `is_adult_clothing_confident`

Output:
- local memory object, then persisted into item folder

#### Stage C. Filtering and normalization
Deterministic local logic validates:
- skip if child/kids/baby
- skip if accessory/bag/hat/scarf/etc.
- keep only adult garments
- normalize text fields
- deduplicate image URLs
- generate item ID / folder name

#### Stage D. Persistence
For each accepted item:
- create `data/items/<item_id>/`
- save `metadata.json`
- save `raw_text.txt`
- save `tags.json`
- download images into `images/`
- append status logs into JSONL state files

This split keeps `browser-use` responsible for “understanding pages,” while the local program handles reliability and resuming.

---

### 3. Why browser-use is the right core

Ordinary Playwright parsers usually force you to:
- inspect every site manually,
- write custom selectors,
- maintain lots of exceptions,
- repair breakages when layouts change.

Here, we want the opposite:
- `browser-use` should navigate semantically,
- select product pages based on instructions,
- extract structured output into a schema,
- stay constrained to trusted domains.

That lines up well with the documented `output_model_schema` / structured output flow and with domain restriction via `allowed_domains`. citeturn158976search0turn158976search1

So the design decision is:

**No traditional parser logic for product interpretation.**  
`browser-use` decides:
- what is a product page,
- whether the item is adult clothing,
- what text is useful,
- which images are product photos.

Local code only:
- stores,
- resumes,
- deduplicates,
- validates,
- downloads.

---

### 4. Storage design without a database

You asked for a simple resume-friendly solution. The cleanest option is **JSONL state files**, not a DB.

#### State files
- `state/discovered_urls.jsonl`
- `state/processed_urls.jsonl`
- `state/saved_items.jsonl`
- `state/downloaded_images.jsonl`
- `state/errors.jsonl`

Each line is an append-only JSON object.

Why this is the right choice:
- easy to inspect manually
- append-only = robust if process is interrupted
- no DB migrations
- no lock headaches
- works well for long scraping jobs

Example semantics:
- `discovered_urls.jsonl`: candidate URLs discovered from listing pages
- `processed_urls.jsonl`: pages already attempted successfully or intentionally skipped
- `saved_items.jsonl`: accepted products saved locally
- `downloaded_images.jsonl`: map image URL → local path
- `errors.jsonl`: recoverable failures

This gives you stop/resume behavior with minimal operational complexity.

---

### 5. Item folder structure

Per item:

```text
data/
  items/
    <item_id>/
      metadata.json
      raw_text.txt
      tags.json
      images/
        001.jpg
        002.jpg
        003.jpg
```

#### `metadata.json`
Should contain at least:
- `item_id`
- `source_site`
- `product_url`
- `title`
- `gender`
- `category`
- `subcategory`
- `season`
- `garment_elements`
- `pattern_description`
- `useful_text_excerpt`
- `image_source_urls`
- `downloaded_images`
- `scraped_at`
- `language`
- `is_adult_clothing_confident`

This makes every item self-contained and reusable for later indexing, retrieval, tagging, or training.

---

### 6. Discovery strategy

This is where most bad scrapers become fragile.

We should not hardcode CSS selectors across all sites from the beginning. Instead:

#### Discovery prompt for browser-use
For each site, instruct the agent to:
- start from homepage or obvious pattern/catalog section
- navigate only within allowed domain
- find pages that appear to be **individual product pages**
- prioritize women’s and men’s clothing pattern pages
- exclude:
  - children
  - kids
  - baby
  - accessories
  - bags
  - hats
  - scarves
  - decor
  - editorial/blog-only pages
- return absolute URLs only
- deduplicate URLs

Because `browser-use` supports `allowed_domains`, we can keep the agent safely constrained to the intended site. citeturn158976search1turn158976search3

#### Important engineering rule
Discovery and extraction should be separate runs logically:
- discovery gathers candidate URLs
- extraction processes candidate URLs

This separation lets you:
- stop after discovery,
- inspect candidate quality,
- rerun extraction independently.

---

### 7. Extraction strategy

For each candidate URL:

`browser-use` should return a **typed product schema**.

#### Extraction prompt should ask for:
- exact product URL
- product title
- adult clothing or not
- gender target: women / men / unisex / unknown
- category and subcategory
- season
- design/construction elements
- useful descriptive text
- text about the pattern
- list of URLs of finished-garment photos from multiple angles if available
- flags:
  - `is_child_item`
  - `is_accessory`
  - `is_adult_clothing_confident`

#### Why schema-based extraction matters
It makes the system:
- easier to validate,
- easier to test,
- easier to improve later,
- much better than scraping free text and post-processing chaos.

This is directly supported by `browser-use` structured output. citeturn158976search0

---

### 8. Deterministic local validation layer

Even with smart extraction, add a local deterministic validator.

It should reject items if:
- `is_child_item == true`
- `is_accessory == true`
- title/text contains obvious banned markers
- there are no product images
- there is no meaningful garment information
- URL duplicates an already saved item

This is not “ordinary parsing”; it is **post-extraction quality control**.

That gives you a hybrid system:
- semantic extraction by browser-use
- deterministic acceptance rules locally

This is the senior-engineering version of robust scraping.

---

### 9. Image download logic

You do **not** need to download patterns/files themselves, only finished-garment photos.

So for each accepted item:
- take the image URLs returned by `browser-use`
- deduplicate them
- optionally cap count, for example 3–8 best images
- download only if not already downloaded

Resume behavior:
- if image file already exists, skip
- if image URL already marked downloaded in state, skip

This prevents wasting bandwidth and time during reruns.

---

### 10. Stop/resume behavior

This is a must-have.

#### Safe stop
At every durable step, append state immediately:
- discovered URL found → write to `discovered_urls.jsonl`
- page processed → write to `processed_urls.jsonl`
- item saved → write to `saved_items.jsonl`
- image downloaded → write to `downloaded_images.jsonl`

Then `Ctrl+C` is safe enough because most progress is already flushed.

#### Resume
On startup:
- load all state files into memory sets
- skip discovered URLs already processed
- skip items already saved
- skip images already downloaded

This gives true resumability without a DB.

Yes, create a predefined tag vocabulary first.

That is better than letting tags drift randomly.

#### Tag generation rule
Do it in two phases:
1. local base vocabulary
2. browser-use or model-assisted selection from the vocabulary plus a few normalized extras when truly needed

That keeps tags consistent.

### 12. Site-specific adaptation plan

Even though we are avoiding hand-written Playwright parsers, you still want **site-aware prompting**.

Not CSS selectors — prompt strategy.

For each site create a small config:
- start URL
- domain allowlist
- discovery instruction
- preferred catalog areas if known
- language hint
- likely blockers:
  - blogs
  - magazines
  - tutorials
  - mixed content
  - article pages

Example:
- `korfiati.ru` may contain more tutorial/editorial-style content
- `burdastyle.ru` can mix editorial/materials with actual patterns
- `simplicity.com` / `marfy.it` may be more catalog-like

So instead of site-specific parsing code, create:
- site-specific discovery prompts
- site-specific extraction emphasis
- site-specific negative filters

That is much cheaper to maintain.

---

### 13. Quality evaluation plan

Before scaling to full crawl, do a staged evaluation.

#### Phase 1: smoke test
Run 10–20 items per site.

Measure:
- precision of “true adult clothing pattern” items
- image usefulness
- metadata completeness
- duplicate rate
- skip rate
- error rate

#### Phase 2: prompt tuning
Adjust:
- discovery prompts
- extraction schema wording
- negative filters
- tag mapping

#### Phase 3: scaled crawl
Run higher limits only after precision is acceptable.

This saves a lot of wasted crawling.

### 14. Observability and debugging

Add simple logs:
- current site

Also save a small structured crawl report:

This matters because LLM-agent pipelines can fail in non-obvious ways if you do not log carefully.

### 15. Security and token handling

Use:
- `.env`

### 17. Implementation order

Best order to build this:

1. create clean repo
2. add config and `.env` support
3. add state manager using JSONL
4. implement one generic browser-use discovery agent

My recommendation is:

**Use browser-use for these three things only**
- discovering product pages
- extracting structured product data

And use ordinary local Python for:

- downloads

That gives you the best tradeoff:
- smart enough to avoid brittle selectors

```python
    gender: Optional[str] = None  # women / men / unisex / unknown
    category: Optional[str] = None
    subcategory: Optional[str] = None

    season: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
    garment_elements: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
    materials: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)

    short_description: str = ""
    pattern_info: str = ""
    raw_text: str = ""

    adult_only: bool = True
    is_accessory: bool = False
    is_child_item: bool = False

    images: list[ProductImage] = Field(default_factory=list)


class GeneratedTags(BaseModel):
    tags: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)


# =========================
# TAG VOCABULARY
# =========================

BASE_TAGS = [
    "women", "men", "unisex",
    "dress", "blouse", "shirt", "top", "tshirt", "skirt",
    "trousers", "pants", "jeans", "shorts",
    "jacket", "blazer", "coat", "trench", "vest",
    "hoodie", "sweatshirt", "sweater", "cardigan",
    "jumpsuit", "bodysuit", "loungewear", "sleepwear", "swimwear",
    "fitted", "relaxed", "oversized", "straight", "a-line", "wrap",
    "slim-fit", "wide-leg", "cropped", "maxi", "midi", "mini",
    "sleeveless", "short-sleeve", "long-sleeve", "puff-sleeve",
    "raglan", "dropped-shoulder",
    "v-neck", "crew-neck", "boat-neck", "turtleneck",
    "collar", "lapel", "hood", "zipper", "buttons",
    "pleats", "darts", "pockets", "belt", "lining",
    "casual", "office", "formal", "evening", "classic", "minimalist",
    "spring", "summer", "autumn", "winter", "demi-season",
    "beginner", "intermediate", "advanced",
    "pdf-pattern", "sewing-pattern",
]


# =========================
# UTILS
# =========================

def ensure_dir(path: Path) -> None:
    path.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)


def append_jsonl(path: Path, row: dict) -> None:
    ensure_dir(path.parent)
    with path.open("a", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        f.write(json.dumps(row, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n")


def read_jsonl_keyset(path: Path, key: str) -> set[str]:
    if not path.exists():
        return set()
    result = set()
    with path.open("r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        for line in f:
            line = line.strip()
            if not line:
                continue
            try:
                obj = json.loads(line)
                val = obj.get(key)
                if isinstance(val, str) and val:
                    result.add(val)
            except Exception:
                continue
    return result


def write_json(path: Path, data: dict) -> None:
    ensure_dir(path.parent)
    path.write_text(json.dumps(data, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")


def normalize_tag(tag: str) -> str:
    return re.sub(r"[-\s]+", "-", tag.strip().lower())


def merge_tags(*groups: list[str]) -> list[str]:
    seen = set()
    out = []
    for group in groups:
        for tag in group:
            t = normalize_tag(tag)
            if t and t not in seen:
                seen.add(t)
                out.append(t)
    return out


def slugify(text: str, max_len: int = 120) -> str:
    text = text.lower().strip()
    text = re.sub(r"[^\w\s-]+", " ", text, flags=re.UNICODE)
    text = re.sub(r"[\s_-]+", "-", text, flags=re.UNICODE).strip("-")
    return text[:max_len] or "item"


def stable_item_id(url: str, title: str) -> str:
    host = urlparse(url).netloc.replace("www.", "")
    return f"{slugify(host)}__{slugify(title or url)}"


def dedupe_preserve_order(items: list[str]) -> list[str]:
    seen = set()
    out = []
    for x in items:
        if x and x not in seen:
            seen.add(x)
            out.append(x)
    return out


def is_probably_image_url(url: str) -> bool:
    u = url.lower()
    return any(ext in u for ext in [".jpg", ".jpeg", ".png", ".webp", ".avif"])


def filename_from_url(url: str, fallback: str) -> str:
    name = Path(urlparse(url).path).name or fallback
    if "." not in name:
        name += ".jpg"
    return name[:180]


def detect_site_key(url: str) -> str:
    host = urlparse(url).netloc.lower()
    for key, base in SITE_URLS.items():
        if urlparse(base).netloc.lower().replace("www.", "") in host.replace("www.", ""):
            return key
    return host.replace("www.", "")


# =========================
# STATE
# =========================

@dataclass
class State:
    discovered_urls: set[str]
    processed_urls: set[str]
    saved_item_ids: set[str]
    downloaded_image_urls: set[str]


def load_state() -> State:
    ensure_dir(STATE_DIR)
    return State(
        discovered_urls=read_jsonl_keyset(DISCOVERED_FILE, "url"),
        processed_urls=read_jsonl_keyset(PROCESSED_FILE, "url"),
        saved_item_ids=read_jsonl_keyset(SAVED_ITEMS_FILE, "item_id"),
        downloaded_image_urls=read_jsonl_keyset(DOWNLOADED_IMAGES_FILE, "url"),
    )


# =========================
# BROWSER-USE
# =========================

def build_llm() -> ChatOpenAI:
    load_dotenv()
    api_key = os.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY")
    if not api_key:
        raise RuntimeError("OPENAI_API_KEY is missing. Put it into .env")

    # Use a model you have access to.
    return ChatOpenAI(
        model="gpt-4.1",
        api_key=api_key,
    )


def build_browser() -> Browser:
    # allowed_domains is the important restriction here.
    return Browser(
        headless=True,
        allowed_domains=ALLOWED_DOMAINS,
    )


def make_discovery_prompt(start_url: str, limit: int) -> str:
    return f"""
Open this site: {start_url}

Your goal:
Find individual product pages for adult sewing/clothing patterns.

Important constraints:
- Work ONLY inside the allowed domain.
- Return ONLY concrete product/item pages, not category pages.
- Focus on WOMEN'S and MEN'S clothing.
- Exclude:
  - children / kids / baby / teen
  - accessories
  - bags, hats, scarves, belts, gloves, socks
  - toys, dolls, pets
  - home decor, quilts, crafts
  - articles/blog/editorial pages unless they clearly correspond to one concrete garment pattern page
- Prefer pages with finished garment photos and product/pattern description.
- Return up to {limit} unique absolute URLs.

Output only the structured result.
""".strip()


def make_product_prompt(product_url: str) -> str:
    return f"""
Open this exact product page: {product_url}

Extract structured information for this item.

Rules:
- This should be an adult clothing item or clothing pattern page.
- Exclude children items and accessories.
- product_url must be exactly "{product_url}".
- source_site should be the site key inferred from the domain.
- Collect URLs of finished garment photos from different angles if available.
- Prefer photos of the garment, not technical drawings only.
- Put useful readable page text into raw_text.
- Leave unknown fields empty rather than inventing data.

Output only the structured result.
""".strip()


def make_tags_prompt(card: ProductCard) -> str:
    payload = json.dumps(card.model_dump(mode="json"), ensure_ascii=False, indent=2)
    vocab = ", ".join(BASE_TAGS)
    return f"""
Generate tags for this clothing item.

Allowed tag vocabulary:
{vocab}

Input item:
{payload}

Rules:
- Return 5 to 20 tags.
- lowercase
- hyphen-separated
- no duplicates
- prioritize garment type, fit, details, gender, season, style
- use vocabulary above whenever possible
- if a necessary tag is not in vocabulary, you may add a short normalized tag

Output only the structured result.
""".strip()


async def discover_urls_for_site(start_url: str, limit: int) -> list[str]:
    browser = build_browser()
    agent = Agent(
        task=make_discovery_prompt(start_url, limit),
        llm=build_llm(),
        browser=browser,
        use_vision=True,
        output_model_schema=ListingLinks,
        max_failures=3,
    )
    result = await agent.run()

    data = None
    if hasattr(result, "output"):
        data = result.output
    elif hasattr(result, "structured_output"):
        data = result.structured_output

    if not data:
        return []

    urls = dedupe_preserve_order(data.listing_urls)
    return urls[:limit]


async def extract_product(product_url: str) -> Optional[ProductCard]:
    browser = build_browser()
    agent = Agent(
        task=make_product_prompt(product_url),
        llm=build_llm(),
        browser=browser,
        use_vision=True,
        output_model_schema=ProductCard,
        max_failures=3,
    )
    result = await agent.run()

    data = None
    if hasattr(result, "output"):
        data = result.output
    elif hasattr(result, "structured_output"):
        data = result.structured_output

    return data


async def generate_tags(card: ProductCard) -> list[str]:
    browser = build_browser()
    agent = Agent(
        task=make_tags_prompt(card),
        llm=build_llm(),
        browser=browser,
        use_vision=False,
        output_model_schema=GeneratedTags,
        max_failures=2,
    )
    result = await agent.run()

    data = None
    if hasattr(result, "output"):
        data = result.output
    elif hasattr(result, "structured_output"):
        data = result.structured_output

    if not data:
        return []
    return dedupe_preserve_order(data.tags)


# =========================
# VALIDATION
# =========================

def is_valid_product(card: ProductCard) -> bool:
    if card.is_accessory or card.is_child_item:
        return False

    text = " ".join([
        card.title or "",
        card.category or "",
        card.subcategory or "",
        card.short_description or "",
        card.pattern_info or "",
        card.raw_text or "",
    ]).lower()

    banned = [
        "kids", "child", "children", "baby", "teen",
        "аксессуар", "accessory", "bag", "hat", "cap", "scarf",
        "glove", "sock", "toy", "doll", "pet",
        "сумк", "шапк", "шарф", "перчат",
    ]
    if any(x in text for x in banned):
        return False

    positive = [
        "dress", "blouse", "shirt", "top", "skirt",
        "trousers", "pants", "jeans", "shorts",
        "jacket", "blazer", "coat", "vest", "hoodie",
        "sweater", "cardigan", "jumpsuit", "bodysuit",
        "плать", "блуз", "рубаш", "топ", "юбк",
        "брюк", "брюки", "джинс", "шорт", "жакет",
        "пиджак", "пальто", "жилет", "худи", "свитер",
        "кардиган", "комбинез",
    ]
    if not any(x in text for x in positive):
        return False

    if not card.images:
        return False

    return True


# =========================
# DOWNLOADS
# =========================

async def download_one_image(client: httpx.AsyncClient, url: str, target: Path) -> bool:
    try:
        r = await client.get(url, timeout=60.0, follow_redirects=True)
        r.raise_for_status()
        ctype = r.headers.get("content-type", "").lower()
        if "image" not in ctype and not is_probably_image_url(url):
            return False
        target.write_bytes(r.content)
        return True
    except Exception:
        return False


async def download_images(card: ProductCard, item_dir: Path, state: State, max_images: int) -> list[str]:
    images_dir = item_dir / "images"
    ensure_dir(images_dir)

    image_urls = dedupe_preserve_order([img.url for img in card.images if img.url])[:max_images]
    saved_paths: list[str] = []

    async with httpx.AsyncClient(headers={"User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0"}) as client:
        for idx, url in enumerate(image_urls, start=1):
            if STOP_REQUESTED:
                break

            if url in state.downloaded_image_urls:
                existing = images_dir / filename_from_url(url, f"image_{idx}.jpg")
                if existing.exists():
                    saved_paths.append(str(existing))
                continue

            target = images_dir / filename_from_url(url, f"image_{idx}.jpg")
            if target.exists():
                saved_paths.append(str(target))
                state.downloaded_image_urls.add(url)
                append_jsonl(DOWNLOADED_IMAGES_FILE, {"url": url, "path": str(target)})
                continue

            ok = await download_one_image(client, url, target)
            if ok:
                saved_paths.append(str(target))
                state.downloaded_image_urls.add(url)
                append_jsonl(DOWNLOADED_IMAGES_FILE, {"url": url, "path": str(target)})

    return saved_paths


# =========================
# SAVE ITEM
# =========================

def save_item(card: ProductCard, tags: list[str], downloaded_images: list[str]) -> str:
    item_id = stable_item_id(card.product_url, card.title)
    item_dir = ITEMS_DIR / item_id
    ensure_dir(item_dir)

    metadata = {
        "item_id": item_id,
        "source_site": card.source_site,
        "product_url": card.product_url,
        "title": card.title,
        "gender": card.gender,
        "category": card.category,
        "subcategory": card.subcategory,
        "season": card.season,
        "garment_elements": card.garment_elements,
        "materials": card.materials,
        "short_description": card.short_description,
        "pattern_info": card.pattern_info,
        "adult_only": card.adult_only,
        "is_accessory": card.is_accessory,
        "is_child_item": card.is_child_item,
        "image_source_urls": [img.url for img in card.images],
        "downloaded_images": downloaded_images,
    }

    write_json(item_dir / "metadata.json", metadata)
    write_json(item_dir / "tags.json", {"tags": tags})
    (item_dir / "raw_text.txt").write_text(card.raw_text or "", encoding="utf-8")

    return item_id


# =========================
# MAIN PIPELINE
# =========================

async def discover_phase(state: State, limit_per_site: int) -> None:
    for site_key, start_url in SITE_URLS.items():
        if STOP_REQUESTED:
            return

        print(f"[DISCOVER] {site_key} -> {start_url}", flush=True)
        try:
            urls = await discover_urls_for_site(start_url, limit_per_site)
            added = 0
            for url in urls:
                if url not in state.discovered_urls:
                    state.discovered_urls.add(url)
                    append_jsonl(DISCOVERED_FILE, {"url": url, "site": site_key})
                    added += 1
            print(f"[DISCOVER] {site_key}: found={len(urls)} new={added}", flush=True)
        except Exception as e:
            append_jsonl(ERRORS_FILE, {"phase": "discover", "site": site_key, "error": str(e)})
            print(f"[DISCOVER] {site_key}: error={e}", flush=True)


async def process_phase(state: State, max_images: int) -> None:
    queue = list(state.discovered_urls)

    total = len(queue)
    print(f"[PROCESS] queued={total}", flush=True)

    for idx, url in enumerate(queue, start=1):
        if STOP_REQUESTED:
            return

        if url in state.processed_urls:
            continue

        print(f"[PROCESS] [{idx}/{total}] {url}", flush=True)

        try:
            card = await extract_product(url)
            if not card:
                append_jsonl(ERRORS_FILE, {"phase": "extract", "url": url, "error": "no structured output"})
                continue

            if not card.source_site:
                card.source_site = detect_site_key(url)

            if not card.product_url:
                card.product_url = url

            item_id = stable_item_id(card.product_url, card.title)

            # skip if already saved
            if item_id in state.saved_item_ids:
                state.processed_urls.add(url)
                append_jsonl(PROCESSED_FILE, {"url": url, "status": "already_saved"})
                continue

            if not is_valid_product(card):
                state.processed_urls.add(url)
                append_jsonl(PROCESSED_FILE, {"url": url, "status": "filtered_out"})
                continue

            item_dir = ITEMS_DIR / item_id
            downloaded = await download_images(card, item_dir, state, max_images=max_images)
            tags = merge_tags(await generate_tags(card))

            saved_item_id = save_item(card, tags, downloaded)

            state.saved_item_ids.add(saved_item_id)
            append_jsonl(SAVED_ITEMS_FILE, {"item_id": saved_item_id, "url": url})

            state.processed_urls.add(url)
            append_jsonl(PROCESSED_FILE, {"url": url, "status": "saved"})

            print(f"[PROCESS] saved -> {saved_item_id}", flush=True)

        except Exception as e:
            append_jsonl(ERRORS_FILE, {"phase": "process", "url": url, "error": str(e)})
            print(f"[PROCESS] error -> {e}", flush=True)
            # do not mark processed on hard failure


async def async_main():
    ensure_dir(DATA_DIR)
    ensure_dir(STATE_DIR)
    ensure_dir(ITEMS_DIR)

    # very simple CLI
    limit_per_site = DEFAULT_LIMIT_PER_SITE
    max_images = DEFAULT_MAX_IMAGES

    if len(sys.argv) > 1:
        for i, arg in enumerate(sys.argv):
            if arg == "--limit-per-site" and i + 1 < len(sys.argv):
                limit_per_site = int(sys.argv[i + 1])
            elif arg == "--max-images" and i + 1 < len(sys.argv):
                max_images = int(sys.argv[i + 1])

    state = load_state()

    print("[START] Discovery phase", flush=True)
    await discover_phase(state, limit_per_site=limit_per_site)

    if STOP_REQUESTED:
        print("[STOP] Interrupted after discovery.", flush=True)
        return

    print("[START] Process phase", flush=True)
    await process_phase(state, max_images=max_images)

    print("[DONE]", flush=True)


def main():
    asyncio.run(async_main())


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
```

## 4) How it works

This version uses **one shared prompt template** for:
- discovery across all 8 sites
- extraction from any product page
- tag generation

The state is stored in **JSONL** files:
- `data/_state/discovered_urls.jsonl`
- `data/_state/processed_urls.jsonl`
- `data/_state/saved_items.jsonl`
- `data/_state/downloaded_images.jsonl`
- `data/_state/errors.jsonl`

That means you can:
- run it,
- stop with `Ctrl+C`,
- run again,
- and it will skip URLs already in `processed_urls.jsonl` and images already in `downloaded_images.jsonl`.

Each item is saved into:
```text
data/items/<item_id>/
  metadata.json
  raw_text.txt
  tags.json
  images/
```

## 7) Restart behavior

When you stop the program:
- discovered URLs already written stay saved
- processed URLs already written stay saved
- downloaded image URLs already written stay saved

So the next launch does **not** need to revisit previously processed pages.

Important detail:
- pages that fail with a hard exception are **not** marked processed, so they can be retried on the next run
- pages filtered out as kids/accessories are marked processed so they do not waste time again

---

## 8) What you may need to tweak

The only thing that may need a small adjustment is the exact `browser-use` import/runtime shape, because their docs currently show structured output support and schema support, but the exact class signatures can vary across versions or between cloud/open-source docs. The design here matches the documented approach: typed schema output plus domain restriction. citeturn731940search0turn731940search2turn731940search6

If your installed version differs, the most likely tiny fixes are:
- `result.output` vs `result.structured_output`
- `browser=` vs `browser_session=`
- browser constructor arguments

The scraper logic itself does not need to change.

```python
from __future__ import annotations

import asyncio
import json
import os
import re
import signal
import sys
from dataclasses import dataclass
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Optional
from urllib.parse import urlparse

import httpx
from dotenv import load_dotenv
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field

from browser_use import Agent, Browser, ChatOpenAI


# =========================
# CONFIG
# =========================

SITE_URLS = {
    "simplicity": "https://simplicity.com/",
    "vikisews": "https://vikisews.com/",
    "burdastyle": "https://burdastyle.ru/",
    "helpersew": "https://helpersew.com/",
    "grasser": "https://grasser.ru/",
    "shkatulka": "https://shkatulka-sew.ru/",
    "korfiati": "https://korfiati.ru/",
    "marfy": "https://www.marfy.it/",
}

ALLOWED_DOMAINS = [
    "simplicity.com",
    "vikisews.com",
    "burdastyle.ru",
    "helpersew.com",
    "grasser.ru",
    "shkatulka-sew.ru",
    "korfiati.ru",
    "marfy.it",
]

DATA_DIR = Path("data")
STATE_DIR = DATA_DIR / "_state"
ITEMS_DIR = DATA_DIR / "items"

DISCOVERED_FILE = STATE_DIR / "discovered_urls.jsonl"
PROCESSED_FILE = STATE_DIR / "processed_urls.jsonl"
SAVED_ITEMS_FILE = STATE_DIR / "saved_items.jsonl"
DOWNLOADED_IMAGES_FILE = STATE_DIR / "downloaded_images.jsonl"
ERRORS_FILE = STATE_DIR / "errors.jsonl"
IN_PROGRESS_FILE = STATE_DIR / "in_progress_urls.jsonl"

DEFAULT_LIMIT_PER_SITE = 80
DEFAULT_MAX_IMAGES = 8
DEFAULT_CONCURRENCY = 4

STOP_REQUESTED = False


# =========================
# SIGNALS
# =========================

def _handle_stop(signum, frame):
    global STOP_REQUESTED
    STOP_REQUESTED = True
    print("\n[!] Stop requested. Waiting for current tasks to finish safely...", flush=True)


signal.signal(signal.SIGINT, _handle_stop)
signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, _handle_stop)


# =========================
# MODELS
# =========================

class ListingLinks(BaseModel):
    listing_urls: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)


class ProductImage(BaseModel):
    url: str
    alt: Optional[str] = None


class ProductCard(BaseModel):
    source_site: str = ""
    product_url: str = ""
    title: str

    gender: Optional[str] = None
    category: Optional[str] = None
    subcategory: Optional[str] = None

    season: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
    garment_elements: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
    materials: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)

    short_description: str = ""
    pattern_info: str = ""
    raw_text: str = ""

    adult_only: bool = True
    is_accessory: bool = False
    is_child_item: bool = False

    images: list[ProductImage] = Field(default_factory=list)


class GeneratedTags(BaseModel):
    tags: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)


# =========================
# TAGS
# =========================

BASE_TAGS = [
    "women", "men", "unisex",
    "dress", "blouse", "shirt", "top", "tshirt", "skirt",
    "trousers", "pants", "jeans", "shorts",
    "jacket", "blazer", "coat", "trench", "vest",
    "hoodie", "sweatshirt", "sweater", "cardigan",
    "jumpsuit", "bodysuit", "loungewear", "sleepwear", "swimwear",
    "fitted", "relaxed", "oversized", "straight", "a-line", "wrap",
    "slim-fit", "wide-leg", "cropped", "maxi", "midi", "mini",
    "sleeveless", "short-sleeve", "long-sleeve", "puff-sleeve",
    "raglan", "dropped-shoulder",
    "v-neck", "crew-neck", "boat-neck", "turtleneck",
    "collar", "lapel", "hood", "zipper", "buttons",
    "pleats", "darts", "pockets", "belt", "lining",
    "casual", "office", "formal", "evening", "classic", "minimalist",
    "spring", "summer", "autumn", "winter", "demi-season",
    "beginner", "intermediate", "advanced",
    "pdf-pattern", "sewing-pattern",
]


# =========================
# UTILS
# =========================

def ensure_dir(path: Path) -> None:
    path.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)


def append_jsonl(path: Path, row: dict) -> None:
    ensure_dir(path.parent)
    with path.open("a", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        f.write(json.dumps(row, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n")


def read_jsonl_keyset(path: Path, key: str) -> set[str]:
    if not path.exists():
        return set()
    result = set()
    with path.open("r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        for line in f:
            line = line.strip()
            if not line:
                continue
            try:
                obj = json.loads(line)
                val = obj.get(key)
                if isinstance(val, str) and val:
                    result.add(val)
            except Exception:
                continue
    return result


def write_json(path: Path, data: dict) -> None:
    ensure_dir(path.parent)
    path.write_text(json.dumps(data, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")


def normalize_tag(tag: str) -> str:
    return re.sub(r"[-\s]+", "-", tag.strip().lower())


def merge_tags(*groups: list[str]) -> list[str]:
    seen = set()
    out = []
    for group in groups:
        for tag in group:
            t = normalize_tag(tag)
            if t and t not in seen:
                seen.add(t)
                out.append(t)
    return out


def slugify(text: str, max_len: int = 120) -> str:
    text = text.lower().strip()
    text = re.sub(r"[^\w\s-]+", " ", text, flags=re.UNICODE)
    text = re.sub(r"[\s_-]+", "-", text, flags=re.UNICODE).strip("-")
    return text[:max_len] or "item"


def stable_item_id(url: str, title: str) -> str:
    host = urlparse(url).netloc.replace("www.", "")
    return f"{slugify(host)}__{slugify(title or url)}"


def dedupe_preserve_order(items: list[str]) -> list[str]:
    seen = set()
    out = []
    for x in items:
        if x and x not in seen:
            seen.add(x)
            out.append(x)
    return out


def is_probably_image_url(url: str) -> bool:
    u = url.lower()
    return any(ext in u for ext in [".jpg", ".jpeg", ".png", ".webp", ".avif"])


def filename_from_url(url: str, fallback: str) -> str:
    name = Path(urlparse(url).path).name or fallback
    if "." not in name:
        name += ".jpg"
    return name[:180]


def detect_site_key(url: str) -> str:
    host = urlparse(url).netloc.lower().replace("www.", "")
    for key, base in SITE_URLS.items():
        base_host = urlparse(base).netloc.lower().replace("www.", "")
        if base_host in host:
            return key
    return host


def get_structured_output(result):
    if hasattr(result, "output") and result.output is not None:
        return result.output
    if hasattr(result, "structured_output") and result.structured_output is not None:
        return result.structured_output
    if hasattr(result, "final_result") and result.final_result is not None:
        return result.final_result
    return None


# =========================
# STATE
# =========================

@dataclass
class State:
    discovered_urls: set[str]
    processed_urls: set[str]
    saved_item_ids: set[str]
    downloaded_image_urls: set[str]
    in_progress_urls: set[str]


def load_state() -> State:
    ensure_dir(STATE_DIR)
    return State(
        discovered_urls=read_jsonl_keyset(DISCOVERED_FILE, "url"),
        processed_urls=read_jsonl_keyset(PROCESSED_FILE, "url"),
        saved_item_ids=read_jsonl_keyset(SAVED_ITEMS_FILE, "item_id"),
        downloaded_image_urls=read_jsonl_keyset(DOWNLOADED_IMAGES_FILE, "url"),
        in_progress_urls=read_jsonl_keyset(IN_PROGRESS_FILE, "url"),
    )


# =========================
# BROWSER-USE
# =========================

def build_llm() -> ChatOpenAI:
    load_dotenv()
    api_key = os.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY")
    if not api_key:
        raise RuntimeError("OPENAI_API_KEY is missing in .env")

    return ChatOpenAI(
        model="gpt-4.1",
        api_key=api_key,
    )


def build_browser() -> Browser:
    return Browser(
        headless=True,
        allowed_domains=ALLOWED_DOMAINS,
    )


def make_discovery_prompt(start_url: str, limit: int) -> str:
    return f"""
Open this site: {start_url}

Your goal:
Find individual product pages for adult sewing/clothing patterns.

Important constraints:
- Work ONLY inside the allowed domain.
- Return ONLY concrete product/item pages, not category pages.
- Focus on WOMEN'S and MEN'S clothing.
- Exclude:
  - children / kids / baby / teen
  - accessories
  - bags, hats, scarves, belts, gloves, socks
  - toys, dolls, pets
  - home decor, quilts, crafts
  - articles/blog/editorial pages unless they clearly correspond to one concrete garment pattern page
- Prefer pages with finished garment photos and product/pattern description.
- Return up to {limit} unique absolute URLs.

Output only the structured result.
""".strip()


def make_product_prompt(product_url: str) -> str:
    return f"""
Open this exact product page: {product_url}

Extract structured information for this item.

Rules:
- This should be an adult clothing item or clothing pattern page.
- Exclude children items and accessories.
- product_url must be exactly "{product_url}".
- source_site should be the site key inferred from the domain.
- Collect URLs of finished garment photos from different angles if available.
- Prefer photos of the garment, not technical drawings only.
- Put useful readable page text into raw_text.
- Leave unknown fields empty rather than inventing data.

Output only the structured result.
""".strip()


def make_tags_prompt(card: ProductCard) -> str:
    payload = json.dumps(card.model_dump(mode="json"), ensure_ascii=False, indent=2)
    vocab = ", ".join(BASE_TAGS)
    return f"""
Generate tags for this clothing item.

Allowed tag vocabulary:
{vocab}

Input item:
{payload}

Rules:
- Return 5 to 20 tags.
- lowercase
- hyphen-separated
- no duplicates
- prioritize garment type, fit, details, gender, season, style
- use vocabulary above whenever possible
- if a necessary tag is not in vocabulary, you may add a short normalized tag

Output only the structured result.
""".strip()


async def discover_urls_for_site(start_url: str, limit: int) -> list[str]:
    browser = build_browser()
    try:
        agent = Agent(
            task=make_discovery_prompt(start_url, limit),
            llm=build_llm(),
            browser=browser,
            use_vision=True,
            output_model_schema=ListingLinks,
            max_failures=3,
        )
        result = await agent.run()
        data = get_structured_output(result)
        if not data:
            return []
        return dedupe_preserve_order(data.listing_urls)[:limit]
    finally:
        maybe_close = getattr(browser, "close", None)
        if callable(maybe_close):
            maybe = maybe_close()
            if asyncio.iscoroutine(maybe):
                await maybe


async def extract_product(product_url: str) -> Optional[ProductCard]:
    browser = build_browser()
    try:
        agent = Agent(
            task=make_product_prompt(product_url),
            llm=build_llm(),
            browser=browser,
            use_vision=True,
            output_model_schema=ProductCard,
            max_failures=3,
        )
        result = await agent.run()
        return get_structured_output(result)
    finally:
        maybe_close = getattr(browser, "close", None)
        if callable(maybe_close):
            maybe = maybe_close()
            if asyncio.iscoroutine(maybe):
                await maybe


async def generate_tags(card: ProductCard) -> list[str]:
    browser = build_browser()
    try:
        agent = Agent(
            task=make_tags_prompt(card),
            llm=build_llm(),
            browser=browser,
            use_vision=False,
            output_model_schema=GeneratedTags,
            max_failures=2,
        )
        result = await agent.run()
        data = get_structured_output(result)
        if not data:
            return []
        return dedupe_preserve_order(data.tags)
    finally:
        maybe_close = getattr(browser, "close", None)
        if callable(maybe_close):
            maybe = maybe_close()
            if asyncio.iscoroutine(maybe):
                await maybe


# =========================
# FILTERING
# =========================

def is_valid_product(card: ProductCard) -> bool:
    if card.is_accessory or card.is_child_item:
        return False

    text = " ".join([
        card.title or "",
        card.category or "",
        card.subcategory or "",
        card.short_description or "",
        card.pattern_info or "",
        card.raw_text or "",
    ]).lower()

    banned = [
        "kids", "child", "children", "baby", "teen",
        "аксессуар", "accessory", "bag", "hat", "cap", "scarf",
        "glove", "sock", "toy", "doll", "pet",
        "сумк", "шапк", "шарф", "перчат",
    ]
    if any(x in text for x in banned):
        return False

    positive = [
        "dress", "blouse", "shirt", "top", "skirt",
        "trousers", "pants", "jeans", "shorts",
        "jacket", "blazer", "coat", "vest", "hoodie",
        "sweater", "cardigan", "jumpsuit", "bodysuit",
        "плать", "блуз", "рубаш", "топ", "юбк",
        "брюк", "брюки", "джинс", "шорт", "жакет",
        "пиджак", "пальто", "жилет", "худи", "свитер",
        "кардиган", "комбинез",
    ]
    if not any(x in text for x in positive):
        return False

    if not card.images:
        return False

    return True


# =========================
# DOWNLOADS
# =========================

async def download_one_image(client: httpx.AsyncClient, url: str, target: Path) -> bool:
    try:
        r = await client.get(url, timeout=60.0, follow_redirects=True)
        r.raise_for_status()
        ctype = r.headers.get("content-type", "").lower()
        if "image" not in ctype and not is_probably_image_url(url):
            return False
        target.write_bytes(r.content)
        return True
    except Exception:
        return False


async def download_images(card: ProductCard, item_dir: Path, state: State, max_images: int) -> list[str]:
    images_dir = item_dir / "images"
    ensure_dir(images_dir)

    image_urls = dedupe_preserve_order([img.url for img in card.images if img.url])[:max_images]
    saved_paths: list[str] = []

    async with httpx.AsyncClient(headers={"User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0"}) as client:
        for idx, url in enumerate(image_urls, start=1):
            if STOP_REQUESTED:
                break

            target = images_dir / filename_from_url(url, f"image_{idx}.jpg")

            if target.exists():
                saved_paths.append(str(target))
                if url not in state.downloaded_image_urls:
                    state.downloaded_image_urls.add(url)
                    append_jsonl(DOWNLOADED_IMAGES_FILE, {"url": url, "path": str(target)})
                continue

            if url in state.downloaded_image_urls:
                continue

            ok = await download_one_image(client, url, target)
            if ok:
                saved_paths.append(str(target))
                state.downloaded_image_urls.add(url)
                append_jsonl(DOWNLOADED_IMAGES_FILE, {"url": url, "path": str(target)})

    return saved_paths


# =========================
# SAVE ITEM
# =========================

def save_item(card: ProductCard, tags: list[str], downloaded_images: list[str]) -> str:
    item_id = stable_item_id(card.product_url, card.title)
    item_dir = ITEMS_DIR / item_id
    ensure_dir(item_dir)

    metadata = {
        "item_id": item_id,
        "source_site": card.source_site,
        "product_url": card.product_url,
        "title": card.title,
        "gender": card.gender,
        "category": card.category,
        "subcategory": card.subcategory,
        "season": card.season,
        "garment_elements": card.garment_elements,
        "materials": card.materials,
        "short_description": card.short_description,
        "pattern_info": card.pattern_info,
        "adult_only": card.adult_only,
        "is_accessory": card.is_accessory,
        "is_child_item": card.is_child_item,
        "image_source_urls": [img.url for img in card.images],
        "downloaded_images": downloaded_images,
    }

    write_json(item_dir / "metadata.json", metadata)
    write_json(item_dir / "tags.json", {"tags": tags})
    (item_dir / "raw_text.txt").write_text(card.raw_text or "", encoding="utf-8")

    return item_id


# =========================
# PIPELINE
# =========================

async def discover_phase(state: State, limit_per_site: int) -> None:
    for site_key, start_url in SITE_URLS.items():
        if STOP_REQUESTED:
            return

        print(f"[DISCOVER] {site_key} -> {start_url}", flush=True)
        try:
            urls = await discover_urls_for_site(start_url, limit_per_site)
            added = 0
            for url in urls:
                if url not in state.discovered_urls:
                    state.discovered_urls.add(url)
                    append_jsonl(DISCOVERED_FILE, {"url": url, "site": site_key})
                    added += 1
            print(f"[DISCOVER] {site_key}: found={len(urls)} new={added}", flush=True)
        except Exception as e:
            append_jsonl(ERRORS_FILE, {"phase": "discover", "site": site_key, "error": str(e)})
            print(f"[DISCOVER] {site_key}: error={e}", flush=True)


async def process_one_url(url: str, state: State, max_images: int, lock: asyncio.Lock) -> None:
    if STOP_REQUESTED:
        return

    # Reserve URL first so another concurrent worker does not take it.
    async with lock:
        if url in state.processed_urls or url in state.in_progress_urls:
            return
        state.in_progress_urls.add(url)
        append_jsonl(IN_PROGRESS_FILE, {"url": url})

    try:
        print(f"[PROCESS] {url}", flush=True)

        card = await extract_product(url)
        if not card:
            append_jsonl(ERRORS_FILE, {"phase": "extract", "url": url, "error": "no structured output"})
            return

        if not card.source_site:
            card.source_site = detect_site_key(url)
        if not card.product_url:
            card.product_url = url

        item_id = stable_item_id(card.product_url, card.title)

        async with lock:
            if item_id in state.saved_item_ids:
                state.processed_urls.add(url)
                append_jsonl(PROCESSED_FILE, {"url": url, "status": "already_saved"})
                return

        if not is_valid_product(card):
            async with lock:
                state.processed_urls.add(url)
                append_jsonl(PROCESSED_FILE, {"url": url, "status": "filtered_out"})
            return

        item_dir = ITEMS_DIR / item_id
        downloaded = await download_images(card, item_dir, state, max_images=max_images)
        tags = merge_tags(await generate_tags(card))
        saved_item_id = save_item(card, tags, downloaded)

        async with lock:
            state.saved_item_ids.add(saved_item_id)
            append_jsonl(SAVED_ITEMS_FILE, {"item_id": saved_item_id, "url": url})
            state.processed_urls.add(url)
            append_jsonl(PROCESSED_FILE, {"url": url, "status": "saved"})
            print(f"[SAVED] {saved_item_id}", flush=True)

    except Exception as e:
        append_jsonl(ERRORS_FILE, {"phase": "process", "url": url, "error": str(e)})
        print(f"[ERROR] {url} -> {e}", flush=True)

    finally:
        async with lock:
            state.in_progress_urls.discard(url)


async def process_phase_concurrent(state: State, max_images: int, concurrency: int) -> None:
    queue = [
        url for url in state.discovered_urls
        if url not in state.processed_urls and url not in state.in_progress_urls
    ]
    print(f"[PROCESS] queued={len(queue)} concurrency={concurrency}", flush=True)

    lock = asyncio.Lock()
    semaphore = asyncio.Semaphore(concurrency)

    async def runner(url: str):
        async with semaphore:
            if STOP_REQUESTED:
                return
            await process_one_url(url, state, max_images, lock)

    tasks = []
    for url in queue:
        if STOP_REQUESTED:
            break
        tasks.append(asyncio.create_task(runner(url)))

    if not tasks:
        return

    try:
        await asyncio.gather(*tasks)
    except asyncio.CancelledError:
        pass


async def async_main():
    ensure_dir(DATA_DIR)
    ensure_dir(STATE_DIR)
    ensure_dir(ITEMS_DIR)

    limit_per_site = DEFAULT_LIMIT_PER_SITE
    max_images = DEFAULT_MAX_IMAGES
    concurrency = DEFAULT_CONCURRENCY

    argv = sys.argv[1:]
    i = 0
    while i < len(argv):
        if argv[i] == "--limit-per-site" and i + 1 < len(argv):
            limit_per_site = int(argv[i + 1])
            i += 2
        elif argv[i] == "--max-images" and i + 1 < len(argv):
            max_images = int(argv[i + 1])
            i += 2
        elif argv[i] == "--concurrency" and i + 1 < len(argv):
            concurrency = int(argv[i + 1])
            i += 2
        else:
            i += 1

    state = load_state()

    print("[START] Discovery phase", flush=True)
    await discover_phase(state, limit_per_site=limit_per_site)

    if STOP_REQUESTED:
        print("[STOP] Interrupted after discovery.", flush=True)
        return

    print("[START] Concurrent process phase", flush=True)
    await process_phase_concurrent(state, max_images=max_images, concurrency=concurrency)

    print("[DONE]", flush=True)


def main():
    asyncio.run(async_main())


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
```

## What changed in this version

The key change is **concurrent processing of product pages**:
- discovery is still site-by-site
- extraction/saving runs with multiple workers
- URLs are reserved in memory with `in_progress_urls`
- processed URLs are still written to JSONL
- already processed pages are skipped on restart

So if you stop the program and start again, it will not go back to pages already in `processed_urls.jsonl`. That is the main resume guarantee.

## Important note

Because `browser-use` versions can differ a bit, the two places that might need tiny adjustment are:
- how the structured result is exposed after `run()`
- exact browser constructor options

But the documented pattern of `output_model_schema` and typed structured output is supported. citeturn414323search0turn414323search1

## Recommended settings

Start with:
```bash
python main.py --limit-per-site 20 --max-images 5 --concurrency 2
```

Then increase once it looks good.

## Practical caveat

This code prevents revisiting **processed** pages.  
If you kill the process while a page is mid-processing, that URL may be retried on the next run unless it had already reached the processed state. That is usually the right behavior, because otherwise you could lose unfinished items.

The `in_progress_urls.jsonl` file is only there to avoid double work inside one run. On a fresh restart, unfinished URLs are intentionally eligible again.

Next, I can make it a bit tougher by adding:
- retry with backoff,
- per-site timeout budgets,
- better duplicate detection across near-identical titles.

That error is happening **before your prompts matter**. It means `browser-use` is failing to attach to a Chromium CDP endpoint, so discovery never really starts. Browser Use’s docs show that open-source usage relies on Chromium/CDP, and their setup recommends installing `browser-use` plus its browser runtime; their Playwright integration example also shows CDP usage and explicitly installs Chromium. citeturn539235search0turn774584view2turn774584view0

The two most likely causes are:
1. the browser runtime was not installed correctly, or
2. your installed `browser-use` version expects a `BrowserSession`/CDP flow different from the `Browser(...)` code path I gave you. There is also at least one reported browser-use issue with this exact “did not receive a valid HTTP response” CDP failure. citeturn539235search0turn774584view1turn774584view2

## Important code fix

Use **`BrowserSession` instead of `Browser`** for the agent session. In current browser-use docs and examples, `Agent(..., browser_session=...)` is the safer pattern than passing `browser=...` directly for this kind of workflow. Their examples also show `BrowserSession(cdp_url=...)` for CDP-backed sessions. citeturn774584view2turn539235search3

```python
from __future__ import annotations

from typing import Optional
from urllib.parse import urlparse

from browser_use import Agent, BrowserSession, ChatOpenAI


SITE_URLS = {

    "burdastyle": "https://burdastyle.ru/",
    "helpersew": "https://helpersew.com/",

}

ALLOWED_DOMAINS = [
    "*.simplicity.com",
    "*.vikisews.com",
    "*.burdastyle.ru",
    "*.helpersew.com",
    "*.grasser.ru",
    "*.shkatulka-sew.ru",
    "*.korfiati.ru",
    "*.marfy.it",
]

DATA_DIR = Path("data")
STATE_DIR = DATA_DIR / "_state"
ITEMS_DIR = DATA_DIR / "items"

DISCOVERED_FILE = STATE_DIR / "discovered_urls.jsonl"
PROCESSED_FILE = STATE_DIR / "processed_urls.jsonl"

DOWNLOADED_IMAGES_FILE = STATE_DIR / "downloaded_images.jsonl"

DEFAULT_LIMIT_PER_SITE = 40

def _handle_stop(signum, frame):

    STOP_REQUESTED = True
    print("\n[!] Stop requested. Finishing current tasks safely...", flush=True)


signal.signal(signal.SIGINT, _handle_stop)
signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, _handle_stop)


class ListingLinks(BaseModel):
    listing_urls: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)


class ProductImage(BaseModel):

    alt: Optional[str] = None


class ProductCard(BaseModel):

    product_url: str = ""

    category: Optional[str] = None
    subcategory: Optional[str] = None

    season: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
    garment_elements: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
    materials: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)

    short_description: str = ""
    pattern_info: str = ""
    raw_text: str = ""

    adult_only: bool = True
    is_accessory: bool = False
    is_child_item: bool = False

    images: list[ProductImage] = Field(default_factory=list)
```
